articles.summary: STYLO [6] is an AI assistant that lives inside every Zendesk ticket to solve your 10-MINUTE TICKETS IN 10 SECONDS. Stylo automatically creates answers from your Guide Help Center, translates messages, standardizes agent tone, and more. Customers get (correct) answers fast; agents do less gruntwork 🙌

articles.title: TRY STYLO INSTANTLY: SEE STYLO SOLVE A TICKET (NO SIGNUP REQUIRED)
THE OPENAI KEYNOTE
articles.summary: Sam Altman took to the stage to announce new products and features available from OpenAI at the company’s first-ever developer conference. Here’s a long take that breaks down the talk and the key takeaways.

articles.title: MORE GOOD NEWS IN STARTUPLAND
articles.summary: DataDog announced earnings that beat plans. In its earnings call, it mentions that it’s seeing signs that the cost-cutting activities that dominated the start of this year are plateauing, which hopefully acts as a signal of good news for tech.

articles.title: HOW AN INVESTOR MIGHT CATEGORIZE YOUR AI STARTUP
CAN MOST “PRETTY GOOD” SALES REPS SELL ANY SAAS PRODUCT?
articles.summary: Probably not. When hiring salespeople, dig into these points. Known brands are easier to sell, and enterprise SaaS is meaningfully different from SaaS built for smaller businesses. Only the very best salespeople can context switch between SaaS effectively.

articles.title: YOUR BUSINESS MODEL DRIVES YOUR MEASUREMENT
articles.summary: Usage-based pricing seems to be rising in popularity. It’s a revenue model that scales value for both parties as a customer uses your product more. What metric you decide to pivot your pricing around is hard to land on. This metric is commonly called the “Value Metric”. Here’s a breakdown of metrics used by some top companies.

articles.title: THE BRAINTRUST: WHAT SHOULD YOU HAVE INVESTED IN SOONER?
articles.summary: Reflections from a diverse set of 8 unicorn founders on things they might have done differently earlier on in building their businesses.

articles.title: CHARGEBACKSTOP (PRODUCT)
articles.summary: Automatically detect and prevent chargebacks in your Stripe account.

articles.title: RE:CATCH (PRODUCT)
articles.summary: Automatically qualify, route, and schedule inbound leads to supercharge your sales pipeline.

articles.title: SOFTWARE MARKET GENES
articles.summary: Software building on top of open-source LLMs, how defensible is that? A lot of AI, much like vertical SaaS, is a winner-take-most market. Investors and founders are racing to reach an inflection point that brings down their CAC and makes market capture increasingly easier.

articles.title: TAKE TIME TO PROCESS A FAILURE BEFORE STARTING A NEW COMPANY
articles.summary: Shutting down a company often comes with a grieving process, just like a breakup or the loss of a loved one. Give yourself time to process before jumping back into the startup fray – investors tend to watch out for clear rebounds.

articles.title: TRANSPARENCY IS HARD TO DO WELL
articles.summary: Teams with high trust often function more efficiently. Trust requires a degree of transparency, but transparency can be unsettling without context. How you share information is just as important as what you share.

articles.title: A JOB IS JUST A PHASE IN SOMEONE’S LIFE
articles.summary: Managers and employees often play this bizarre game where they pretend the employee will be working at the company forever. That’s never the case and the company won’t be around forever either. Honest conversations about career ambitions will help with retention and trust building.

articles.title: 11 HOURS SINCE OPENAI DEV DAY: HERE IS WHAT PEOPLE HAVE ALREADY BUILT
articles.summary: If you haven’t seen the news about OpenAI Dev Day yet, this thread gives a good estimation of what is now possible. It’s pretty mind-blowing.

articles.title: EVEN THE SMARTEST VCS SOMETIMES GET IT WRONG - BILL GURLEY AND REGULATED MARKETS
articles.summary: Bill Gurley gave an impassioned talk at the All-In Summit a few weeks ago that has been held in high regard. Here to give the counterpoint, Steve Blank lays out why he thinks Gurley might have gotten it wrong. Gurley’s talk and Blank’s rebuttal are both well said.
